items.headline: Rivian Tops Q1 Estimates, but Investors Investors Rightly Remain Wary
items.summary: Electric vehicle stocks have faced a bumpy road lately, with softening demand, tariff worries, and high interest rates testing even the strongest players. Investors wonder if newer entrants like Rivian can scale fast enough to compete against Tesla’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) volume machine or Ford’s (NYSE:F) established truck network. Rivian’s (NASDAQ:RIVN) latest quarterly results offer a mixed ... Rivian Tops Q1 Estimates, but Investors Investors Rightly Remain Wary

items.headline: Why Growth Investors Are Ditching FELG for the NASDAQ-100’s 9.74% Edge
items.summary: Growth investors picking a large-cap vehicle in 2026 face a real choice: pay nothing for a passive index, or pay a few basis points for an active manager who claims to add value. The Fidelity Enhanced Large Cap Growth ETF (NYSEARCA:FELG) sits squarely in that second camp, charging 0.18% for an actively managed take on ... items.headline: Corning Meta Fiber Expansion Deepens Ties To AI Data Center Growth
items.summary: Corning (NYSE:GLW) and Meta have started a $6b expansion of Corning's optical cable plant in North Carolina. The project is intended to support AI data center growth and strengthen US based fiber supply. The expansion represents a multi year commitment between Corning and a large technology partner focused on AI infrastructure. Corning, trading at $158.26, has seen very strong share price performance, with the stock up 74.5% year to date and 251.1% over the past year. The new expansion with...
